task_completion.description: Open the Save As dialog in Subsurface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' or 'Export' file dialog window is open over the Subsurface application.
The file dialog shows the current directory as 'Documents' or '/home/ga/Documents/'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct filename for the database
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename input field in the save dialog contains exactly 'dives_database.sqlite'.
The file type or format dropdown in the save dialog is set to 'Subsurface SQLite database' or shows the '*.sqlite' extension.

task_completion.description: Confirm the file was saved successfully
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog is closed and the Subsurface main window title updates to reflect the new filename ('dives_database.sqlite'), OR a file manager/terminal window shows the 'dives_database.sqlite' file present in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: Agent used the Subsurface application to save the file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface graphical user interface, specifically its file menus and save dialogs.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'sqlite3', python scripts, or other CLI tools being used to convert the XML file to SQLite.

integrity.description: Agent did not create an empty or fake file using a text editor or basic file manager commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen using tools like 'touch', 'nano', 'gedit', or right-click 'Create Document' to simply make a blank file named 'dives_database.sqlite'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/dives_database.sqlite'. The VLM must verify that this exact filename and directory are used in the Subsurface save dialog. The original XML file contains 29 dives, which will be preserved in the SQLite database if saved correctly through the application.
